seoTitle: Combine Two Documents Into One in Canva
metaDescription: Learn how to merge two separate Canva documents into a single cohesive file to simplify your workflow and keep branding consistent.
intro: Merging documents in Canva helps you keep related content together and maintain a consistent look across your materials. This guide covers how to combine two separate documents into one cohesive design.

steps.heading: Review both documents
steps.body: Open the two Canva documents you want to merge and note the pages or elements you want to carry over.

steps.heading: Copy pages between documents
steps.body: Use Canva's copy or duplicate page options to move content from one document into the other.

steps.heading: Maintain branding consistency
steps.body: Check fonts, colors, and layout across the combined pages so the final document feels cohesive rather than pieced together.

steps.heading: Finalize your merged document
steps.body: Review the full combined document for order and flow before saving or exporting the final version.

faq.question: Can I combine documents of different sizes?
faq.answer: It's best to match page sizes before combining, since mismatched dimensions can create inconsistent layouts.

faq.question: Will combining documents affect the original files?
faq.answer: No, copying pages between documents doesn't delete the originals unless you choose to remove them afterward.

faq.question: Is there a limit to how many pages I can combine?
faq.answer: Canva allows multi-page documents, though very large combined files may take longer to load or edit.

faq.question: Can AI help suggest layout improvements after combining?
faq.answer: Canva's design suggestion tools can offer layout tips, though final adjustments are still up to you for the best result.
